definition: Reserve diversification away from USTs is structural, not cyclical. Foreign official holdings declining from 33% to 23%. EM central bank gold purchases at 1,037-1,045 tonnes/year (2023-2024), representing $65-75B of reallocation. The marginal buyer shifting from price-insensitive reserve managers to price-sensitive basis traders increases velocity of any fiscal repricing. This is a volatility regime change, not just a level shift.
evidence: Foreign official UST holdings declining from 33% to 23%
EM central bank gold purchases at 1,037-1,045 tonnes/year
Marginal buyer shift from price-insensitive to price-sensitive
Pair_1 highlighted volatility regime change framing
open_questions: At what gold allocation (currently 5-8%, targeting 10-15%) does EM central bank buying decelerate?
Does the marginal buyer shift create a nonlinear vulnerability to supply shocks?
